channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.description: Dynamos move closer to appointing a highly rated European coach as negotiations advance, raising hopes for a new era. The post Dynamos Edge Closer to Appointing European Top Coach appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #Tech #AfricaTech #ZimbabweNews

item.title: CCC senators blow hot and cold on bill to extend Mnangagwa rule

item.description: The battle for influence around President Emmerson Mnangagwa has shifted to Manicaland, where a growing rivalry between businessmen Kudakwashe Tagwirei and Paul Tungwarara is exposing factional tensio... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Zanu-PF MPs scrambling to defend CAB 3 lawsuits @bulawayo24 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: ZANU PF is scrambling to defend an avalanche of lawsuits filed by voters against its Members of Parliament following their support for the controversial Constitution Amendment No. 3 Bill (CAB 3), as o... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Govt spokesperson calls former Botswana President a “washed-up has-been” for criticising controversial CAB3
item.description: GOVERNMENT spokesperson Ndabaningi Mangwana has called former Botswana President Ian Khama a washed-up has-been for criticising President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s controversial Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3). CAB3 seeks to extend Mnangagwa’s stay in office, lengthen the life of Parliament and totally do away with Presidential elections by introducing a system where legislators will be the [...] The post Govt spokesperson calls former Botswana President a “washed-up has-been” for criticising controversial CAB3 appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe ConCourt leaves referendum door open on CAB 3 @bulawayo24
item.description: The Constitutional Court (ConCourt) has left open the possibility that the controversial Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB 3) could still be subjected to a national referendum, even as the legi... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.description: Zimbabwean motorists planning to import vehicles should brace for increased scrutiny from the Zimbabwe Revenue Authority (Zimra) as authorities move to tighten compliance and close tax loopholes in a... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Economists warn CAB 3 could hurt Zimbabwe investor confidence @bulawayo24
item.description: MOUNTING political tensions surrounding the Constitution Amendment No. 3 Bill (CAB 3) are beginning to spill over into economic discourse, with economists warning that the controversy could undermine... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: You will be burnt to death: Honourable Amos Chibaya warned over CAB 3 meeting
item.description: HARARE – In a chilling escalation of political tensions, former opposition legislator Honourable Amos Chibaya has reported receiving a death threat, explicitly warning him against proceeding with a planned political meeting. The ominous message, delivered anonymously, stated: “Chimeeting chamuri kuronga chamangwana chikangoitwa uri kutsvira mumba” – a Shona phrase that translates to, “If the meeting [...] The post You will be burnt to death: Honourable Amos Chibaya warned over CAB 3 meeting first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: GMB to set up 1 800 collection points and 89 depots @zimsituation
item.description: Source: GMB to set up 1 800 collection points and 89 depots – herald Dr Anxious Masuka Raymond Jaravaza, THE Grain Marketing Board (GMB) will set up 1 804 collection points and 89 depots for a convenient and cost-effective mechanism that will allow farmers to timeously deliver maize and traditional grains for a successful 2025/2026 [...] The post GMB to set up 1 800 collection points and 89 depots appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Dynamos coach Genesis Mangombe suspended for using juju and making sure ‘Dembare either loses matches or draws’
item.description: Dynamos Coach Embroiled in Juju and Match-Fixing Scandal HARARE – The world of Zimbabwean football has been rocked by the immediate suspension of Dynamos FC head coach, Genesis Mangombe, amidst a cascade of serious allegations. The charges, which include insubordination, the controversial use of juju, and deeply troubling claims of match-fixing, have cast a long [...] The post Dynamos coach Genesis Mangombe suspended for using juju and making sure ‘Dembare either loses matches or draws’ first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe eyes bigger share of regional market as intra-SADC trade remains low
item.description: ZIMBABWE has outlined plans to capitalise on the low levels of intra-SADC trade, which currently account for just 21% of total trade within the regional bloc. Presenting a report on meetings of the SADC Committee of Ministers of Trade and the Ministerial Taskforce on Regional Economic Integration, Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Trade, Professor [...] The post Zimbabwe eyes bigger share of regional market as intra-SADC trade remains low appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: From Harare to Utah: Sidney Sithole Investigates HIV Mechanisms
item.description: BYU College of Life Sciences Growing up in Zimbabwe, where an HIV diagnosis was often a death sentence, Sidney Sithole (MMBIO ’27) became determined to understand how a small virus, only around 120 nanometers, could cause such devastation. He spent five years working with the University of Zimbabwe Clinical Trials Research Center (UZ-CTRC) as a medical [...] The post From Harare to Utah: Sidney Sithole Investigates HIV Mechanisms appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
